name: cattrs
what_it_does: Python library for structuring (deserializing) and unstructuring (serializing) complex Python objects. Built on top of attrs but also works with dataclasses. Converts between untyped dicts/lists and typed attrs/dataclass instances using Python type hints. Highly customizable via Converter class. The complement to attrs for serialization/deserialization.
use_cases: Deserialize JSON API responses into typed attrs or dataclass objects using cattrs.structure() with full type hint support
Serialize complex Python object graphs to dicts/JSON with customizable field mapping using cattrs.unstructure()
Handle Union types and Optional fields in deserialization with automatic type disambiguation
Build custom converters for domain-specific serialization needs (date formats, enum handling, custom type coercion)
Validate and deserialize configuration files (JSON, TOML, YAML) into strongly-typed Python objects
not_for: Pydantic-style schema generation or FastAPI integration — use Pydantic for OpenAPI schema and API validation
Simple JSON parsing where dicts suffice — avoid adding cattrs for trivial cases that don't need typed objects
High-performance binary serialization — use msgpack or protobuf for binary formats
best_when: You're using attrs or dataclasses and need round-trip serialization/deserialization with type safety and customization.
avoid_when: You need JSON schema, OpenAPI integration, or are starting fresh — Pydantic covers both validation and serialization in one package.

auth.notes: Pure Python library — no auth needed.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structure failures raise cattrs.ClassValidationError with a list of all field errors (not just the first). Unstructure is generally safe. Error messages include field paths for nested structures.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pure transformation library — all operations are deterministic given the same input.
known_agent_gotchas: cattrs v22+ changed from Converter to BaseConverter/Converter split — code using old Converter may need updates when upgrading
structure() is strict about extra keys by default in newer versions — configure converter with forbid_extra_keys=False if input dicts have unknown fields
Union type structuring requires type tags or custom disambiguation hooks — simple Union[A, B] won't auto-discriminate without configuration
ClassValidationError collects ALL errors, not just the first — agents should iterate through .exceptions attribute for full error list
cattrs does not validate the output of unstructure() — serialization is assumed correct; validation only happens on structure()
The global cattrs.structure/unstructure uses a global converter — use explicit Converter() instances in library code to avoid global state mutation

security_score_components.security_notes: Pure transformation library — no network access, no security concerns beyond input validation.
